skill.name: render-automation
skill.description: Automate Render cloud platform operations via Rube MCP (Composio). Manage services, trigger deployments, monitor status, and organize projects through documented MCP tool workflows.
skill.summary: Automate Render services and deployments via Rube MCP integration
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: This is a documentation-only skill containing no executable code. The static findings flagged markdown backticks and URL references as potential security issues, but these are false positives. The skill provides documentation for using Rube MCP tools to manage Render services - no shell execution, network requests, or cryptographic operations occur. All detected patterns are documentation artifacts.

content.value_statement: This skill automates Render cloud platform operations including service management, deployment triggering, and status monitoring through the Rube MCP integration.
actual_capabilities: List and filter Render services by name, type, or pagination
Trigger manual deployments for any Render service
Monitor deployment status and retrieve build logs
limitations: Requires Rube MCP server to be configured with Render toolkit
Cannot create new Render services - only manages existing ones
Deployment polling intervals should respect API rate limits
Requires active Render account with connected authentication

use_cases.title: Deploy applications to Render
use_cases.description: Trigger and monitor deployments for web services, static sites, workers, or cron jobs from Claude or Codex

use_cases.title: Monitor deployment status
use_cases.description: Check deployment progress, retrieve build logs, and verify successful deployments

use_cases.title: Manage Render infrastructure
use_cases.description: List services, filter by type, and organize projects across Render account

prompt_templates.title: List all web services
prompt_templates.prompt: List all web services on my Render account using the render-automation skill.

prompt_templates.title: Deploy a specific service
prompt_templates.prompt: Trigger a deployment for my service called 'my-app' and monitor until it completes.

prompt_templates.title: Check deployment status
prompt_templates.prompt: What's the current status of deployment dep-xxxx for service srv-xxxx?

prompt_templates.title: List all projects
prompt_templates.prompt: Show me all Render projects and their associated services.

output_examples.input: List all web services on Render
output_examples.output:
Found 5 web services:
- my-app (srv-abc123) - status: live
- api-service (srv-def456) - status: live
- worker-queue (srv-ghi789) - status: update_in_progress

Deployment triggered successfully.
- Service: my-app (srv-abc123)
- Deploy ID: dep-xyz789
- Status: build_in_progress

Deployment Status: live
- Created: 2024-01-15T10:30:00Z
- Finished: 2024-01-15T10:45:00Z
- Commit: abc1234
- Build: SUCCESS

best_practices: Always call RUBE_SEARCH_TOOLS first to get current tool schemas before use
Use pagination cursors when listing services to avoid missing results
Poll deployment status at 10-30 second intervals to respect rate limits
anti_patterns: Do not trigger multiple deployments simultaneously without checking queue status
Do not assume deployment succeeded without checking status for 'live'
Do not skip service ID resolution - always use LIST_SERVICES to get correct IDs

faq.question: What is Rube MCP?
faq.answer: Rube MCP is a Model Context Protocol server by Composio that provides tools for interacting with cloud services like Render. It must be configured separately from this skill.

faq.question: Do I need a Render account?
faq.answer: Yes, you need an active Render account. The skill requires authentication through Rube MCP which connects to your Render account.

faq.question: Can this skill create new services?
faq.answer: No, this skill only manages existing services. It can list, deploy, and monitor services but cannot create new ones.

faq.question: How do I find my service ID?
faq.answer: Use RENDER_LIST_SERVICES with the name filter to find your service. The response includes the service ID in format 'srv-xxxxxxxx'.

faq.question: What deployment statuses should I watch for?
faq.answer: 'live' means success. 'build_failed' or 'update_failed' indicate errors. Other statuses like 'build_in_progress' are intermediate states.

faq.question: What rate limits apply?
faq.answer: Render API has rate limits. Poll at 10-30 second intervals and avoid bulk operations in quick succession.
